# The most honest stat on GitHub

**113+ public repos. 12 stars between them — I counted.** Most people would hide that number. I'm leading with it, because *why* I keep shipping anyway is the one thing worth knowing before you scroll — and I'll tell you at the bottom of the tools list.

Everything here is **zero-dependency Python** (stdlib only, MIT-licensed): no bloated dependency trees, no supply-chain surprises, no `pip install` roulette. Built for AI agents, LLM apps, and the developers who babysit them.

---

## 🔥 Today's spotlight

> **🌍 A global situation room with no backend — and no paywall.**
>
> [situroom](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/situroom) — Live earth intel dashboard (the worldmonitor thesis, free). Just shipped. Client-side only. **→ [try it](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/situroom)**

*A different tool steps into this slot every day. Yesterday it was someone else.*

---

## 🎯 Pick your pain

Don't browse 113+ repos. Find your problem, take the tool:

| Your problem right now | The fix | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| 🧨 "My LLM returns broken JSON" | [`jsonsalvage`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/jsonsalvage) | Extracts & repairs JSON from messy LLM output — fences, trailing commas, truncation |
| 🕳️ "What even IS this codebase?" | [`repolens`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/repolens) | X-rays any repo in one command → interactive dependency graph + health grade in a single HTML file |
| 🔥 "I'm burning tokens" | [`tokenseive`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/tokenseive) | Multi-layer token optimization — compress prompts, map codebases, shrink output |
| 🧠 "My agent forgets everything" | [`mnemokit`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/mnemokit) | Persistent agent memory — SQLite + FTS5 full-text search, zero deps |
| 🗺️ "My agent reads whole files to answer one question" | [`codegraph`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/codegraph) | Cached, incremental code-graph maps — agents query structure instead of reading code |
| 🔐 "Secrets keep leaking into prompts" | [`cloakpipe`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/cloakpipe) | Redacts secrets & PII before the API call, restores them in the response. 16 detectors |
| 🎲 "My prompts are guesses" | [`prompt-bench`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/prompt-bench) | Scores prompt quality on 12 dimensions, detects anti-patterns, A/B tests variants |
| 🔌 "I need an MCP server yesterday" | [`mcp-wrap`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/mcp-wrap) | Wraps any REST API into an MCP server in minutes |
| 🚧 "My agent needs guardrails" | [`ratify`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/ratify) | Contract layer for LLM agents — deterministic checks + LLM judgment |
| 🌍 "I want a dashboard, not a backend bill" | [`situroom`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/situroom) | Zero-backend global situation room — live earth intel, 100% client-side |

## ⭐ Flagships

<table align="center">
<tr>
<td width="50%" valign="top">

### 🔍 [repolens](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/repolens)
X-ray any codebase in **one command** — interactive dependency graph, blast radius, health grade, security scan & hotspot map in a single HTML file.

<img src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/nguyenminhduc9988/repolens?style=flat-square&color=6366f1" alt="repolens stars" /> <img src="https://img.shields.io/github/languages/top/nguyenminhduc9988/repolens?style=flat-square&color=22c55e" alt="repolens language" />

</td>
<td width="50%" valign="top">

### 🗺️ [codegraph](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/codegraph)
Cached, incremental code-graph maps so AI agents **query structure instead of reading whole files**. CLI + MCP — works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider.

<img src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/nguyenminhduc9988/codegraph?style=flat-square&color=6366f1" alt="codegraph stars" /> <img src="https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/codegraph-kit?style=flat-square&color=22c55e&label=pypi" alt="codegraph on PyPI" />

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td width="50%" valign="top">

### 🔥 [tokenseive](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/tokenseive)
Multi-layer **token optimization** for LLM apps — compress prompts, map codebases, reduce output. Zero required dependencies.

<img src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/nguyenminhduc9988/tokenseive?style=flat-square&color=6366f1" alt="tokenseive stars" /> <img src="https://img.shields.io/github/languages/top/nguyenminhduc9988/tokenseive?style=flat-square&color=22c55e" alt="tokenseive language" />

</td>
<td width="50%" valign="top">

### 🏰 [guildhall](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/guildhall)
Assemble a **guild of AI agents** that self-builds a roster, self-runs a task DAG, and self-grows a memory of lessons — offline, zero API keys.

<img src="https://img.shields.io/github/stars/nguyenminhduc9988/guildhall?style=flat-square&color=6366f1" alt="guildhall stars" /> <img src="https://img.shields.io/github/languages/top/nguyenminhduc9988/guildhall?style=flat-square&color=22c55e" alt="guildhall language" />

</td>
</tr>
</table>

<details>
<summary><b>📦 The full toolbox — 66+ more zero-dependency tools (click to expand)</b></summary>
<br/>

**🤖 AI agents & LLM infrastructure**

| Repo | What it does |
|------|--------------|
| [`talaria`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/talaria) | The agent runtime that ships — typed deterministic detection + phase-gated resumable pipelines |
| [`guildhall`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/guildhall) | 🏰 A guild of AI agents that self-builds a roster, runs a task DAG, grows a memory of lessons — offline, zero API keys |
| [`agent-loop-lite`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/agent-loop-lite) | Simplified Plan→Act→Observe loop for building agents |
| [`agent-memory`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/agent-memory) | In-memory + file-based multi-layer memory for agents |
| [`signet-lite`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/signet-lite) | Lightweight persistent memory for CLI agents |
| [`context-window`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/context-window) | Smart context-window manager with intelligent compression |
| [`hermes-tool-guard`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/hermes-tool-guard) | Rate-limiting & safety middleware for LLM tool calls |
| [`hookcatch`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/hookcatch) | Local webhook inspector — capture & inspect HTTP requests, nothing leaves 127.0.0.1 |

**🛠️ Developer utilities**

| Repo | What it does |
|------|--------------|
| [`configkeeper`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/configkeeper) | Multi-format config loader |
| [`quickenv`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/quickenv) | Minimal `.env` loader with type casting & validation |
| [`filewatch`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/filewatch) | Cross-platform file/directory change monitor |
| [`shellmate`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/shellmate) | Safe shell-command execution wrapper |
| [`texttools`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/texttools) | Text-processing utilities |
| [`videoforge`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/videoforge) | Automated short-video generation pipeline — script, voiceover, subtitles, composition |

Every one: Python stdlib only. MIT. `pip install` and forget.

</details>

## 🎁 The free shelf

I give the machines away and only sell the factory tours. Start here — it's genuinely enough for most people:

| Free thing | What you get |
|---|---|
| ⚡ [`dev-prompts`](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/dev-prompts) | **120+ copy-paste AI prompts** for engineers — code review, debugging, testing, refactoring. Dependency-free CLI |
| 🧰 [ToolNest](https://github.com/nguyenminhduc9988/free-tools-hub) | **12 free browser tools** — JSON formatter, Base64, password gen, QR & more. 100% client-side, your data never leaves the tab |
| 📚 Everything above | 113+ MIT-licensed repos. Fork them, gut them, ship them in prod. No attribution begging |

**Why give this much away?** Here's the loop from the top closed: every tool on this page runs *daily inside my own autonomous AI agent*.

Stars were never the business model — the tools pay me back in saved hours whether anyone notices or not. You're browsing my actual production stack, not a portfolio.

## 🛒 The store (read this part last)

**Honestly — don't buy anything here until the free shelf fails you.** Most people's use case is covered by the free tools, and I'd rather you star a repo than refund a purchase.
